lecture_title: Poststrukturalisme, diskursiv psykologi og feministisk teori (Forelæsning 20, 2026-04-20)

summary_lines: Forelæsningen samler socialkonstruktionistiske bidrag til personlighedsforståelse.
Kilderne kritiserer individualistiske modeller og fremhæver, at psykologiske realiteter skabes relationelt i sprog og praksis.
Personlighed beskrives som løbende subjektivering, historisk organiseret gennem sociale og politiske ordener.
key_points: Psykologisk viden formes i fælles diskursive praksisser.
Selvet er relationelt konstitueret frem for selvindesluttet.
Psykologiske kategorier er kulturelt og historisk betingede.
Analyse bør følge de praksisser, der producerer delte virkeligheder.

sidecars.relative_path: Readings/W11L1 Poststrukturalisme, diskursiv psykologi og feministisk teori/week.analysis.md
sidecars.content:
## Shared problem
* All three texts fundamentally challenge the traditional ontology of the "self-contained individual" or essentialist "personality." They reject the causal model where an inner, stable trait (e.g., "Personality A") dictates behavior, instead reframing the person

readings.title: Grundbog kapitel 11 - Postpsykologisk subjektiveringsteori
readings.subject_relative_path: Readings/W11L1 Poststrukturalisme, diskursiv psykologi og feministisk teori/W11L1 Grundbog kapitel 11 - Postpsykologisk subjektiveringsteori.pdf

summary_lines: Kapitlet omformulerer personlighed som løbende subjektivering frem for en fast indre kerne.
Det analyserer, hvordan institutioner, normer og diskurser producerer bestemte subjektpositioner.
Handlekraft forstås som situeret forhandling af positioner med mulighed for modstand og forskydning.
key_points: Subjektpositioner er historisk og politisk organiserede.
Identitet er processuel og skabes i praksis.
Forandring sker ved ompositionering i magtrelationer.

sidecars.relative_path: Readings/W11L1 Poststrukturalisme, diskursiv psykologi og feministisk teori/W11L1 Grundbog kapitel 11 - Postpsykologisk subjektiveringsteori.analysis.md
sidecars.content:
## Core distinctions
*   **"Personality" vs. "Subjectivity":** Traditional humanistic psychology treats "personality" as a fixed, internal, and bounded core that causes behavior. Post-psychology replaces this with "subjectivity"—a fluid, decentered, and

readings.title: Gergen (1999)
readings.subject_relative_path: Readings/W11L1 Poststrukturalisme, diskursiv psykologi og feministisk teori/W11L1 Gergen (1999).pdf

summary_lines: Gergen presents a social constructionist view in which psychological realities are generated in relational processes.
He challenges individualist models by emphasizing language, culture, and coordinated action.
The text argues that personhood is historically variable and pragmatically sustained in social life.
key_points: Knowledge claims are shaped by communal practices and discourse.
The self is relationally constituted rather than self-contained.
Psychological categories are culturally contingent and revisable.
Theory should track practices that create shared realities.

sidecars.relative_path: Readings/W11L1 Poststrukturalisme, diskursiv psykologi og feministisk teori/W11L1 Gergen (1999).analysis.md
sidecars.content:
## Core distinctions
*   **Reflexive inquiry vs. Generative theory:** Reflexive inquiry merely critiques and reflects on traditional realities; generative theory actively invents new vocabularies, metaphors, and paradigms to transcend old binaries.
*   **Individualist "

readings.title: Hacking (2007)
readings.subject_relative_path: Readings/W11L1 Poststrukturalisme, diskursiv psykologi og feministisk teori/W11L1 Hacking (2007).pdf

summary_lines: Hacking analyzes how human classifications interact with the people classified, producing looping effects.
Categories in the human sciences can change behavior, self-understanding, and institutional responses.
The text highlights the dynamic co-production of kinds, knowledge, and persons.
key_points: Human kinds are interactive rather than merely natural descriptors.
Classification practices can reshape the classified population.
Institutional uptake stabilizes some categories and destabilizes others.
Ontology and politics of categories are tightly connected.
